comment.body: There were a ton of calls at 5700 before the china deal. It sort of looked like a call spread of 5700/5900. So any reduction in tarriffs would have caused that upside volatility (same to the downside as well). Now that they're all ITM dealers likely aren't selling against the rally as its all hedged, that and all those puts that were getting closed out keep adding to support. If we're going to go down it can't happen until after OPEX imo (entirely because of how we gapped up)

comment.body:
I think I'd add UST's to the macro mix (unexpected, emperor's clothes, source of volatility).
Sticky inflation plus indebtedness plus policy uncertainty; we saw weakness in April.
Tax cuts could be like Kwarteng's Gilt yields bump in '21.
Also, might be used as an instrument of influence, like tariffs, and/or utilized/handled in unexpected ways.
So not risk free, as mostly priced at present.
No idea on timing or probabilities of any of this.
(This is all taken from Ben Hunt's epsilon theory.)

comment.body:
Right that’s exactly what I’m talking about. You’re placing the sole reason for the tariffs existing upon the Russia/Ukraine conflict. It seems a bit misguided. Why don’t you talk about it anymore? The US has dropped out of peace negotiations and now the war is just lingering there.

The war started in 2022. It’s now 2025 and SPX has rallied 40% since it started. Why do equity markets care about it now? Trump also issued tariffs in his first term, when there was no war.

I just don’t understand what is leading you to that conclusion.

It was like the markets couldn’t rally without a peace deal, and that narrative was a major reason why you were hesitant and very cautious when we were at 4800.

We are now 1,000 points higher, and there is no peace deal.

Actually evidences seem to support Alma's claim; now that Ukraine-Russia negotiation should start this week, tariff on China is paused. Now that Trump is in the Middle East; clearly a closer US-Arab alliance is emerging. On the tech front, even the CEOs of almost all the major tech companies are present in this trip...

These countries are the major investors of the US, without which the stock market is just a gigantic nationwide computer game.

The story goes back to volatility, the perplexing controlled decay in February; one had to wonder, why?

comment.body:
How can it be proven that “investors” didn’t buy the bottom or haven’t bought the rally?

How do we really know? Also, a majority of us are traders here. So I don’t care whether it’s investors or if it’s “artificial”. The market has rallied over 1,000 points.

It really doesn’t matter, outside of taking profit right now. Which we aren’t even certain whether we should because the market has already rallied 20% before any of these deals have been fully resolved. Which is why Alma laid out 4 different possible moves going forward.

Seems that the market was rallying on flow and momentum, and not fully on fundamentals (although you know there are insiders who do know the outcomes of these meetings). Which is exactly my point, aren’t we subscribed here to get a better gage on flow, options dynamics, and underlying market conditions?

There were certainly hints with the data that a strong rally was underway, even Alma’s reversion script was flagging buy like crazy at the bottom and the first major retrace.

Just seems like getting sucked too far into narrative driven, subjective fundamentals and overlooking the data and dynamics Alma is supposedly well versed in missed a great opportunity to profit nicely on this rally.

And the fact people here are concerned on it being “artificial” is exactly what’s wrong with this perspective.
